mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or held at an exceptionally low level, the discount-rate channel supports higher equity prices and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed if subsequent 20-day returns are positive after easing episodes and fails if rate cuts coincide with negative index performance.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing episodes do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the mean forward return is negative across a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals worsening labor-market stress, weaker household income growth, and lower expected corporate revenue, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment is accelerating rather than stable.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after triggers is not below the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll income and retail demand, which should support corporate earnings revisions and raise S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is not present when only one of the two series improves.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS and RSAFS improvements do not outperform single-series effects, or if positive 20-day SPX returns are not more frequent when both series rise together.

mechanism_narrative: A short-term improvement in INDPRO after a prior contraction signals a manufacturing rebound and easing supply constraints, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the following week. The effect should be visible only after a clear acceleration in industrial activity.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after being negative
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO inflection points are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative or flat outcomes, or if the average 5-day return is zero or negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST after a weak housing print signals policy-sensitive demand stabilization and higher cyclical growth expectations, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal should fail when housing strength occurs alongside rising unemployment.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change > 5% and UNRATE flat or falling
failure_condition: Reject if housing-strength triggers do not coincide with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal disappears when UNRATE is stable/falling.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS stays very low, real rates remain compressed and investors infer continued accommodation, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is only active when inflation is not being met with policy tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-plus-low-rate triggers fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns, or if returns are negative despite sustained accommodative policy.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate indicates weak consumer income growth and deteriorating risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter rather than just the next month. The effect should be strongest in late-cycle slowdowns, not in early recoveries.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE above 8% and PAYEMS growth below zero
failure_condition: Reject if high-unemployment, falling-payroll periods do not lead to negative 60-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the average return is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates stronger final demand and revenue growth expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The claim fails if GDP growth accelerates without retail-sales improvement or if the market already prices the rebound.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP and retail-sales improvements do not outperform unconditional 60-day SPX returns, or if positive outcomes occur less often than negative outcomes after the joint signal.
